reasoning: Based on multiple reliable sources, by late August 2025 (specifically August 26-27), SpaceX had only conducted 4 Starship launches in 2025 (Flights 7, 8, 9, and 10). Furthermore, only Flight 10 successfully reached space after a series of failures earlier in the year which necessitated a mishap probe by the FAA. To meet the criteria of "10 to 12 SpaceX Starship launches successfully reach an altitude of 62 miles... between Jan 1 2025 and Dec 31 2025", SpaceX would need 9-11 more successful launches in the remaining 4 months of the year. Given their maximum demonstrated cadence (4 launches in 8 months, gaps of months between launches, and FAA investigations after failures), this target is physically and operationally impossible to achieve before December 31, 2025. Therefore, the probability is at the minimum allowed bound.
